title: UltrafastSecp256k1: GPU-accelerated, audit-grade secp256k1 ECC library
description:
High-performance, audit-grade secp256k1 ECC library for Bitcoin and related cryptographic protocols. Constant-time signing matches or modestly exceeds libsecp256k1 on the same compiler (CT-vs-CT, see docs/BENCHMARKS.md). GPU-accelerated kernels for batch verify, BIP-324, BIP-352, and ECDSA/Schnorr signing (CUDA, Metal, OpenCL, ROCm) are provided as out-of-scope optional backends; benchmark methodology and live numbers live in docs/BENCHMARKS.md.

Engineered for an audit-grade trust model: every release ships with a Continuous-Audit-as-Code (CAAS) evidence bundle, 270 exploit proof-of-concept regression tests across 10 audit sections (count grows with CI runs; see CHANGELOG for current totals), formal invariant specifications, multi-CI reproducible-build attestations across GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and Woodpecker (Codeberg), and a documented INTEROP matrix cross-checked against published reference implementations (Wycheproof, libsecp256k1, OpenSSL libcrypto).

License: MIT. Repository: https://github.com/shrec/UltrafastSecp256k1.
references: Wuille, P. et al. BIP-340: Schnorr Signatures for secp256k1 (2020).
Bernstein, D. J. et al. RFC 6979: Deterministic Usage of DSA and ECDSA (2013).
Brown, D. R. L. SEC 2: Recommended Elliptic Curve Domain Parameters v2.0 (2010).
notes: Release archives published on Zenodo are byte-identical to the corresponding GitHub release tags and to the multi-CI reproducible-build attestations under artifacts/reproducible-attestation.json on each provider (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Woodpecker / Codeberg).
